lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Thank you for meeting with me, Stephen, at such short notice.
lines.translation: 感谢你能这么快和我见面，斯蒂芬。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Not a problem, Margaret.
lines.translation: 没问题，玛格丽特。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Now, please give me some good news.
lines.translation: 现在，请给我一些好消息。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Have you agreed to my last proposal?
lines.translation: 你同意我上一个提议了吗？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I have, indeed.
lines.translation: 的确同意了。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: And I wish to sign the agreement pending one small change to be made to the contract.
lines.translation: 我希望签署协议，但合同中有一处小改动。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Margaret, we have been through this for almost a year now, back and forth, making alterations.
lines.translation: 玛格丽特，我们已经为此纠结了快一年了，反反复复地修改。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Are you sure you want to make a sponsorship deal for your clients, or not?
lines.translation: 你到底想不想为你的客户达成这项赞助协议？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I ask this, because, frankly, some people at my end are running out of patience.
lines.translation: 我这么问是因为，坦率地说，我这边有些人的耐心快耗尽了。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I understand your concerns.
lines.translation: 我理解你的担忧。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But as I’m sure you understand, we hold our clients’ best interests to be of the utmost concern.
lines.translation: 但我相信你也明白，我们认为客户的最大利益是首要考虑的事项。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: We therefore come through the fine details of all contracts, rest assured, we all appreciate your firm’s patience.
lines.translation: 因此，我们会仔细审查所有合同的细节，请放心，我们都非常感谢贵公司的耐心。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: OK, fine.
lines.translation: 好吧。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: So what changes do you wish to make?
lines.translation: 那么你想做哪些改动？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Essentially, we would like the new deal to exclude the Middle East.
lines.translation: 基本上，我们希望新协议排除中东地区。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That’s all.
lines.translation: 仅此而已。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: The Middle East.
lines.translation: 中东地区。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Why?
lines.translation: 为什么？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: My client has a couple of other prospective marketing deals from companies in the Middle East.
lines.translation: 我的客户从中东的一些公司那里得到了其他几项潜在的营销协议。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Those offers, should they materialize, would exclusively employ my client’s image in the Middle East only.
lines.translation: 这些提议一旦实现，将仅在中东地区使用我客户的形象。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Therefore, in order to avoid any conflict, we would need to ensure that both marketing campaigns do not overlap geographically.
lines.translation: 因此，为了避免任何冲突，我们需要确保这两项营销活动在地理上不重叠。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What business sector in the Middle East are we talking about here?
lines.translation: 我们这里说的是中东的哪个业务部门？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Real estate.
lines.translation: 房地产。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, that should be okay then.
lines.translation: 哦，那应该没问题。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: So long as the product is very different from our food and beverage market, there should be no conflict of interest.
lines.translation: 只要产品与我们的餐饮市场完全不同，就不应该存在利益冲突。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Nevertheless, I will have to run this through my people.
lines.translation: 尽管如此，我还是得和我的团队沟通一下。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I don’t foresee any problem, though.
lines.translation: 不过我预见不到会有任何问题。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: The Middle East is a negligible market for us, but I still need to check this with a couple of departments.
lines.translation: 中东对我们来说是一个微不足道的市场，但我仍然需要和几个部门核实一下。

lines.speaker: Q1.
lines.text: What does the woman say she will do?
lines.translation: 女人说她会做什么？

lines.speaker: Q2.
lines.text: What does the man say about some people he represents?
lines.translation: 男人关于他所代表的一些人说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q3.
lines.text: What reason does the woman give for the new deal to exclude the Middle East?
lines.translation: 女人给出新协议排除中东的原因是什么？

lines.speaker: Q4.
lines.text: What does the man say about the Middle East?
lines.translation: 男人关于中东说了什么？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Next, we have a special science-related news story.
lines.translation: 接下来，我们有一个特别的科学新闻。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Paula Hancock is at the Denver Observatory.
lines.translation: 保拉·汉考克在丹佛天文台。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Paula, what is the big story over there?
lines.translation: 保拉，那边有什么重大新闻？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Hi, John.
lines.translation: 你好，约翰。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Yes, all the astronomers on site here are very excited.
lines.translation: 是的，这里的全体天文学家都非常兴奋。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: In fact, space enthusiasts all across North America and the rest of the Northern Hemisphere will be congregating on mountain tops tonight to watch the night sky.
lines.translation: 事实上，整个北美以及北半球其他地区的太空爱好者们今晚都将聚集在山顶观看夜空。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Why?
lines.translation: 为什么？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What’s the big event?
lines.translation: 有什么重大活动？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Is there an eclipse happening soon?
lines.translation: 不久后会有日食吗？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Tonight, the Earth will come into close proximity with the Oppenheimer comet.
lines.translation: 今晚，地球将与奥本海默彗星近距离接触。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: It is the closest our planet has been to such a phenomenon in over 100 years.
lines.translation: 这是100多年来我们的星球与这种现象距离最近的一次。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: For this reason, it is expected that thousands of people will gaze up at the sky tonight in order to see this formidable object.
lines.translation: 因此，预计今晚将有成千上万的人仰望天空，以观赏这个庞然大物。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How far away is this comet?
lines.translation: 这颗彗星有多远？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Will people be able to see it with the naked eye?
lines.translation: 人们能用肉眼看到它吗？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: The Oppenheimer comet will still be millions of miles away, on the edge of our galaxy, but nevertheless, this is a relatively close distance, close enough for people to observe in good detail through a telescope.
lines.translation: 奥本海默彗星仍将在数百万英里之外，处于我们星系的边缘，但尽管如此，这仍然是一个相对较近的距离，足以让人们通过望远镜观察到详细的细节。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: People will only see a blur without one.
lines.translation: 没有望远镜的话，人们只能看到一个模糊的光点。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: However, that does not mean one needs professional equipment.
lines.translation: 然而，这并不意味着需要专业设备。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Even the most ordinary of telescopes should be conducive for people to observe and wonder at this flying object.
lines.translation: 即使是最普通的望远镜也应该有助于人们观察和惊叹这个飞行物。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Many of our viewers will be wondering how they too can take part in this once-in-a-lifetime event.
lines.translation: 我们的许多观众都会想知道，他们如何也能参与到这一生一次的盛事中。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Where will this comet be in the sky?
lines.translation: 这颗彗星会出现在天空的哪个位置？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How can people find it?
lines.translation: 人们怎么能找到它？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: The comet will be almost exactly due north, at 60 degrees above the equator.
lines.translation: 彗星将几乎正对着北方，位于赤道上方60度。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: However, finding the comet is indeed very tricky, and scientists here have told me there are plenty of phone apps that will facilitate this.
lines.translation: 然而，寻找这颗彗星确实非常棘手，这里的科学家告诉我，有很多手机应用程序可以提供帮助。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: How fantastic.
lines.translation: 太棒了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Thank you, Paula, for the information.
lines.translation: 谢谢你，保拉，提供的这些信息。

lines.speaker: Q5.
lines.text: What does the woman say about all the astronomers at the Denver Observatory?
lines.translation: 关于丹佛天文台的全体天文学家，女人说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q6.
lines.text: What do we learn from the conversation about the Oppenheimer comet?
lines.translation: 从关于奥本海默彗星的对话中我们了解到了什么？

lines.speaker: Q7.
lines.text: What does the woman say people will only see in the sky without a telescope?
lines.translation: 女人说没有望远镜人们在天空只能看到什么？

lines.speaker: Q8.
lines.text: What do scientists at the Denver Observatory advise amateurs to do to facilitate their observation?
lines.translation: 丹佛天文台的科学家建议业余爱好者做什么来方便他们的观察？

lines.text: Dietary guidelines form the basis for nutrition advice and regulations around the world.
lines.translation: 膳食指南构成了世界各地营养建议和法规的基础。

lines.text: While there is strong scientific consensus around most existing guidelines, one question has recently stirred debate: Should consumers be warned to avoid ultra-processed foods?
lines.translation: 虽然大多数现有指南都有很强的科学共识，但最近有一个问题引起了辩论：是否应该警告消费者避免食用超加工食品？

lines.text: Two papers published today in The American Journal of Clinical Nutrition outline the case for and against using the concept of ultra-processed foods to help inform dietary guidelines beyond conventional food classification systems.
lines.translation: 今天发表在《美国临床营养学杂志》上的两篇论文概述了支持和反对使用超加工食品概念来帮助制定常规食品分类系统之外的膳食指南的理由。

lines.text: The authors, Carlos Monteiro of the University of São Paulo and Arne Astrup of Novo Nordisk Foundation will discuss the issue in a live virtual debate August 14, during NUTRITION 2024 LIVE ONLINE.
lines.translation: 作者，圣保罗大学的卡洛斯·蒙泰罗和诺和诺德基金会的阿恩·阿斯特鲁普，将在8月14日的“营养2024在线直播”期间进行实时虚拟辩论，讨论这一问题。

lines.text: The debate centers around a system developed by Monteiro and colleagues that classifies foods by their degree of industrial processing, ranging from unprocessed to ultra-processed.
lines.translation: 辩论的核心是蒙泰罗及其同事开发的一套系统，该系统根据食品的工业加工程度进行分类，从未经加工到超加工。

lines.text: The system defines ultra-processed foods as those made using sequences of processes that extract substances from foods and alter them with chemicals in order to formulate the final product.
lines.translation: 该系统将超加工食品定义为采用一系列工艺制成的食品，这些工艺从食物中提取物质，并用化学物质改变它们，以配制最终产品。

lines.text: Ultra-processed foods are characteristically designed to be cheap, tasty and convenient; examples include soft drinks and candy, packaged snacks and pastries, ready-to-heat products, and reconstituted meat products.
lines.translation: 超加工食品的特点是设计廉价、美味且方便；例子包括软饮料和糖果、包装零食和糕点、即热产品以及重组肉制品。

lines.text: Studies have linked consumption of ultra-processed foods — which are often high in salt, sugar and fat — with weight gain and an increased risk of chronic diseases, even after adjusting for the amount of salt, sugar and fat in the diet.
lines.translation: 研究将食用超加工食品（通常含有高盐、高糖和高脂肪）与体重增加和慢性疾病风险增加联系起来，即使在调整了饮食中的盐、糖和脂肪含量后也是如此。

lines.text: While the mechanisms behind these associations are not fully understood, Monteiro argues that the existing evidence is sufficient to justify discouraging consumption of ultra-processed foods in dietary recommendations and government policies.
lines.translation: 虽然这些关联背后的机制尚不完全清楚，但蒙泰罗认为，现有证据足以证明在膳食建议和政府政策中不鼓励食用超加工食品是合理的。

lines.speaker: Q9.
lines.text: What question is said to have recently stirred debate?
lines.translation: 据称最近引起辩论的是什么问题？

lines.speaker: Q10.
lines.text: How does the system developed by Monteiro and colleagues classify foods?
lines.translation: 蒙泰罗及其同事开发的系统是如何对食品进行分类的？

lines.speaker: Q11.
lines.text: What is consumption of ultra-processed foods linked with according to studies?
lines.translation: 根据研究，食用超加工食品与什么有关？

lines.text: Believe it or not, human creativity benefits from constraints.
lines.translation: 不管你信不信，人类的创造力受益于约束。

lines.text: According to psychologists, when you have less to work with, you actually begin to see the world differently.
lines.translation: 根据心理学家的说法，当你能利用的资源较少时，你实际上开始以不同的方式看待世界。

lines.text: With constraints, you dedicate your mental energy to acting more resourcefully.
lines.translation: 有了约束，你会把精力集中在更有策略地行动上。

lines.text: When challenged, you figure out new ways to be better.
lines.translation: 当面临挑战时，你会想出新的方法来变得更好。

lines.text: The most successful creative people know that constraints give their minds the impetus to leap higher.
lines.translation: 最成功的创意人才知道，约束给了他们的思维跳得更高的动力。

lines.text: People who invent new products are not limited by what they don’t have or can’t do.
lines.translation: 发明新产品的人并不受限于他们没有什么或不能做什么。

lines.text: They leverage their limitations to push themselves even further.
lines.translation: 他们利用自己的局限性来进一步推动自己。

lines.text: Many products and services are created because the founders saw a limitation in what they use.
lines.translation: 许多产品和服务之所以被创造出来，是因为创始人看到了他们所使用的东西的局限性。

lines.text: They created innovation based on what was not working for them at the moment.
lines.translation: 他们根据当时对他们不起作用的东西创造了创新。

lines.text: Innovation is a creative person’s response to limitation.
lines.translation: 创新是创意人士对局限性的反应。

lines.text: In a 2015 study, which examined how thinking about scarcity or abundance influences how creatively people use their resources, Ravi Mehta at the University of Illinois and Meng Zhu at Johns Hopkins University found that people simply have no incentive to use what’s available to them in novel ways.
lines.translation: 在2015年的一项研究中，伊利诺伊大学的拉维·梅塔和约翰·霍普金斯大学的朱猛研究了关于稀缺或富足的思考如何影响人们创造性地使用资源，他们发现人们根本没有动力以新颖的方式使用他们可获得的资源。

lines.text: When people face scarcity, they give themselves the freedom to use resources in less conventional ways because they have to.
lines.translation: 当人们面临稀缺时，他们会赋予自己以非常规方式使用资源的自由，因为他们不得不这样做。

lines.text: Obstacles can broaden your perception and open up your thinking processes.
lines.translation: 障碍可以拓宽你的感知并开启你的思维过程。

lines.text: Consistent constraints help you improve at connecting unrelated ideas and concepts.
lines.translation: 持续的约束有助于你提高连接无关想法和概念的能力。

lines.text: Marissa Mayer, former vice-president for search products and user experience at Google, once wrote in a publication on Bloomberg.
lines.translation: 谷歌前搜索产品和用户体验副总裁玛丽莎·梅耶尔曾在彭博社的一份出版物中写道。

lines.text: “Constraints shape and focus problems and provide clear challenges to overcome.
lines.translation: “约束塑造并聚焦问题，并提供需要克服的明确挑战。

lines.text: Creativity thrives best when constrained.
lines.translation: 创造力在受到约束时最能蓬勃发展。

lines.speaker: Q12.
lines.text: What do psychologists say people do when they are short of resources?
lines.translation: 心理学家说人们在缺乏资源时会做什么？

lines.speaker: Q13.
lines.text: What does the passage say about innovation?
lines.translation: 文章关于创新说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q14.
lines.text: What did a 2015 study by Ravi Mehta and Meng Zhu find?
lines.translation: 拉维·梅塔和朱猛在2015年的研究中发现了什么？

lines.speaker: Q15.
lines.text: What did Marissa Mayer once write concerning creativity?
lines.translation: 玛丽莎·梅耶尔曾写过关于创造力的什么内容？

lines.text: Different people use different strategies for managing conflicts.
lines.translation: 不同的人使用不同的冲突管理策略。

lines.text: These strategies are learned in childhood.
lines.translation: 这些策略是在童年时期学到的。

lines.text: Usually, we are not aware of how we act in conflict situations.
lines.translation: 通常，我们没有意识到自己在冲突情况下的行为方式。

lines.text: We just do whatever seems to come naturally.
lines.translation: 我们只是做任何看起来自然而然的事情。

lines.text: But we do have a personal strategy; and because it is learned, we can always change it by learning new and more effective ways of managing conflicts.
lines.translation: 但我们确实有一套个人策略；而且因为它是习得的，我们总能通过学习新的、更有效的管理冲突的方法来改变它。

lines.text: When you get involved in a conflict, there are two major concerns you have to take into account: achieving your personal goals and keeping a good relationship with the other person.
lines.translation: 当你卷入冲突时，你必须考虑两个主要问题：实现你的个人目标和与对方保持良好的关系。

lines.text: How important your personal goals are and how important the relationship is to you affect how you act in a conflict.
lines.translation: 你的个人目标有多重要，以及这段关系对你有多重要，会影响你在冲突中的行为方式。

lines.text: Given these two concerns, five styles of managing conflicts can be identified:
lines.translation: 考虑到这两个顾虑，可以确定五种管理冲突的风格：

lines.text: The turtle.
lines.translation: 海龟型。

lines.text: Turtles withdraw into their shells to avoid conflicts.
lines.translation: 海龟退缩到壳里以避免冲突。

lines.text: They give up their personal goals and relationships.
lines.translation: 他们放弃了自己的个人目标和关系。

lines.text: They believe it is easier to withdraw from a conflict than to face it.
lines.translation: 他们认为退出冲突比面对冲突更容易。

lines.text: The shark.
lines.translation: 鲨鱼型。

lines.text: Sharks try to overpower opponents by forcing them to accept their solution to the conflict.
lines.translation: 鲨鱼试图通过强迫对手接受他们的冲突解决方案来压倒对手。

lines.text: They seek to achieve their goals at all costs.
lines.translation: 他们不惜一切代价寻求实现自己的目标。

lines.text: Sharks assume that conflicts are settled by one person winning and one person losing.
lines.translation: 鲨鱼认为冲突是由一个人获胜而另一个人失败来解决的。

lines.text: Winning gives sharks a sense of pride and achievement.
lines.translation: 获胜给鲨鱼一种自豪感和成就感。

lines.text: Losing gives them a sense of weakness, inadequacy and failure.
lines.translation: 失败给他们一种软弱、不足和失败的感觉。

lines.text: The Teddy bear.
lines.translation: 泰迪熊型。

lines.text: Teddy bears want to be accepted and liked by other people.
lines.translation: 泰迪熊想要被别人接受和喜欢。

lines.text: They think that conflicts should be avoided in favor of harmony, and believe that conflicts cannot be discussed without damaging relationships.
lines.translation: 他们认为应该为了和谐而避免冲突，并相信不损害关系就无法讨论冲突。

lines.text: They give up their goals to preserve the relationship.
lines.translation: 他们为了维持关系而放弃了自己的目标。

lines.text: The fox.
lines.translation: 狐狸型。

lines.text: Foxes are moderately concerned with their own goals and about their relationships with other people.
lines.translation: 狐狸中度关注自己的目标以及与他人的关系。

lines.text: They give up part of their goals and persuade the other person in a conflict to give up part of his goals.
lines.translation: 他们放弃一部分目标，并说服冲突中的对方放弃其一部分目标。

lines.text: They seek a solution to conflicts where both sides gain something.
lines.translation: 他们寻求双方都能有所收获的冲突解决方案。

lines.text: The owl.
lines.translation: 猫头鹰型。

lines.text: Owls view conflicts as problems to be solved.
lines.translation: 猫头鹰将冲突视为需要解决的问题。

lines.text: They see conflicts as improving relationships by reducing tension between two people.
lines.translation: 他们认为冲突通过减少两人之间的紧张关系来改善关系。

lines.text: They try to begin a discussion that identifies the conflict as a problem.
lines.translation: 他们试图开始一场将冲突识别为问题的讨论。

lines.text: By seeking solutions that satisfy both themselves and the other person, owls maintain the relationship.
lines.translation: 通过寻求让自己和对方都满意的解决方案，猫头鹰维持了关系。

lines.text: Owls are not satisfied until a solution is found that achieves their own goals and the other person’s goals, and they are not satisfied until the tensions and negative feelings have been fully resolved.
lines.translation: 猫头鹰直到找到实现自己目标和对方目标的解决方案才感到满意，而且直到紧张状态和负面情绪完全消除才感到满意。

lines.speaker: Q16.
lines.text: Why does the speaker say strategies for managing conflicts can always be changed?
lines.translation: 说话者为什么说管理冲突的策略总是可以改变的？

lines.speaker: Q17.
lines.text: What is said to affect the way you want to act in a conflict?
lines.translation: 据称什么会影响你在冲突中想要采取的行为方式？

lines.speaker: Q18.
lines.text: Of the five styles the speaker discusses, which views conflicts as problems to be solved?
lines.translation: 在说话者讨论的五种风格中，哪一种将冲突视为需要解决的问题？

lines.text: The genetic code of all 1.
lines.translation: 所有1.

lines.text: 5 million known species of animals and plants living on earth will be mapped to help save species from extinction and boost human health.
lines.translation: 地球上已知的50万种动植物物种的基因组将被测序，以帮助拯救物种免于灭绝并促进人类健康。

lines.text: Scientists hope that cracking the genetic code of plants and animals could help uncover new treatments for infectious diseases, slow aging, improve crops in agriculture and create new bio-materials.
lines.translation: 科学家希望，破解动植物的基因组可以帮助发现传染病的新疗法、延缓衰老、改善农业作物并创造新的生物材料。

lines.text: In Britain, organizations including the Natural History Museum, the Royal Botanic Gardens at Kew and the Wellcome Sanger Institute have joined forces to sequence Britain’s 66,000 species of animals and plants.
lines.translation: 在英国，包括自然历史博物馆、邱园皇家植物园和维康桑格研究所在内的机构已联手对英国的66,000种动植物进行测序。

lines.text: Dubbed the Darwin Tree of Life Project, it is expected to take 10 years and cost 100 million pounds.
lines.translation: 该项目被称为“达尔文生命之树项目”，预计将耗时10年，耗资1亿英镑。

lines.text: Once completed, all the information will be publicly available to researchers.
lines.translation: 一旦完成，所有信息都将向研究人员公开。

lines.text: Many scientists believe that earth has now entered the sixth mass extinction, with humans creating a toxic mix of habitat loss, pollution and climate change, which has already led to the loss of at least 77 species of mammals and 140 types of birds since 1500.
lines.translation: 许多科学家认为，地球现在已经进入了第六次物种大灭绝，人类造成了栖息地丧失、污染和气候变化的有害组合，自1500年以来，这已经导致了至少77种哺乳动物和140种鸟类的灭绝。

lines.text: It is the biggest loss of species since the dinosaurs were wiped out 66 million years ago.
lines.translation: 这是自6600万年前恐龙灭绝以来最大规模特种损失。

lines.text: Scientists say that sequencing every species will revolutionize the understanding of biology and evolution, bolster efforts to conserve as well as protect and restore biodiversity.
lines.translation: 科学家表示，对每个物种进行测序将彻底改变对生物学和进化的理解，加强保护以及防护和恢复生物多样性的努力。

lines.text: Dr. Tim Littlewood, head of Life Sciences Department at the Natural History Museum, said whether you are interested in food or disease, the history of how every organism on the planet has adapted to its environment is recorded in its genetic makeup.
lines.translation: 自然历史博物馆生命科学部主任蒂姆·利特尔伍德博士说，无论你对食物还是疾病感兴趣，地球上每一个生物如何适应其环境的历史都记录在它的遗传构成中。

lines.text: How you then harness that is dependent on your ability to understand it.
lines.translation: 你如何利用这一点取决于你理解它的能力。

lines.text: We will be using modern methods to get a really good window on the present and the past.
lines.translation: 我们将使用现代方法来获得一扇观察现在和过去的绝佳窗口。

lines.text: And of course, a window on the past gives you a prospective model on the future.
lines.translation: 当然，一扇观察过去的窗口会给你一个关于未来的前瞻性模型。

lines.text: Sir Jim Smith, Director of Science at Wellcome, said, “Try as I might, I can’t think of a more exciting, more relevant, more timely or more internationally inspirational project.
lines.translation: 维康信托基金会科学主任吉姆·史密斯爵士说：“无论我怎么努力，我都想不出一个比这更令人兴奋、更相关、更及时或更具国际启发性的项目了。

lines.text: Since 1970, humanity has wiped out 60% of animal populations.
lines.translation: 自1970年以来，人类已经消灭了60%的动物种群。

lines.text: About 23,000 of 80,000 species surveyed are approaching extinction.
lines.translation: 在接受调查的80,000个物种中，约有23,000个正接近灭绝。

lines.text: We are in the midst of the sixth great extinction event of life on our planet, which not only threatens wildlife species, but also imperils the global food supply.
lines.translation: 我们正处于地球生命第六次大灭绝事件之中，这不仅威胁到野生动物物种，也危及全球粮食供应。

lines.text: As scientists, we all realize we desperately need to catalog life on our fragile planet now.
lines.translation: 作为科学家，我们都意识到我们现在迫切需要为我们脆弱星球上的生命编制目录。

lines.text: I think we’re making history.
lines.translation: 我认为我们正在创造历史。

lines.speaker: Q19.
lines.text: What do scientists hope to do by cracking the genetic code of plants and animals?
lines.translation: 科学家希望通过破解植物和动物的基因组来做什么？

lines.speaker: Q20.
lines.text: What do many scientists believe with regard to earth?
lines.translation: 关于地球，许多科学家相信什么？

lines.speaker: Q21.
lines.text: How does Sir Jim Smith, Director of Science at Welcome, describe the Darwin Tree of Life Project?
lines.translation: 维康信托基金会科学主任吉姆·史密斯爵士如何描述达尔文生命之树项目？

lines.text: John Donne, the English poet, wrote in the 17th century: “No man is an island, entire of itself; every man is a piece of the continent, a part of the main.
lines.translation: 17世纪英国诗人约翰·多恩写道：“没有人是一座孤岛，自成一体；每个人都是大陆的一块，是整体的一部分。

lines.text: ” Now, a British academic has claimed that human individuality is indeed just an illusion, because societies are far more interconnected at a mental, physical and cultural level than people realise.
lines.translation: ”现在，一位英国学者声称人类的个性和独特性确实只是一种幻觉，因为社会在心理、生理和文化层面的相互联系远比人们意识到的要紧密得多。

lines.text: In his new book, The Self Delusion, Professor Tom Oliver, a researcher in the Ecology and Evolution group at the University of Reading, argues there is no such thing as “self” and not even our bodies are truly “us”.
lines.translation: 雷丁大学生态与进化研究小组的研究员汤姆·奥利弗教授在他的新书《自我幻觉》中辩称，根本不存在所谓的“自我”，甚至我们的身体也不是真正的“我们”。

lines.text: Just as Copernicus realised the Earth is not the center of the universe, Professor Oliver said society urgently needs a Copernican-like revolution to understand people are not detached beings but rather part of one connected identity.
lines.translation: 正如哥白尼意识到地球不是宇宙的中心一样，奥利弗教授说，社会迫切需要一场类似哥白尼式的革命，以理解人们不是独立的个体，而是一个相互关联的整体的一部分。

lines.text: “A significant milestone in the cultural evolution of human minds was the acceptance that the Earth is not the center of the universe, the so-called Copernican Revolution,” he writes.
lines.translation: 他写道：“人类思维文化进化的一个重要里程碑是接受了地球不是宇宙的中心，即所谓的哥白尼革命。”

lines.text: “However, we have one more big myth to dispose of: that we exist as independent selves at the center of a subjective universe.
lines.translation: “然而，我们还有一个巨大的神话需要打破：即我们作为独立的自我存在于主观宇宙的中心。

lines.text: You may feel as if you are an independent individual, acting autonomously in the world; that you have unchanging inner self that persists throughout your lifetime, acting as a central anchor-point with the world changing around you.
lines.translation: 你可能觉得你是一个独立的个体，在这个世界上自主地行动；你有一个在你一生中始终不变的内在自我，作为一个中心锚点，而世界在你周围变化。

lines.text: This is the illusion I seek to tackle.
lines.translation: 这就是我试图解决的幻觉。

lines.text: We are intimately connected to the world around us.
lines.translation: 我们与周围的世界紧密相连。

lines.text: ” Professor Oliver argues there are around 37 trillion cells in the body but most have a lifespan of just a few days or weeks, so the material “us” is constantly changing.
lines.translation: ”奥利弗教授辩称，人体内约有37万亿个细胞，但大多数细胞的寿命只有几天或几周，因此物质层面的“我们”是在不断变化的。

lines.text: In fact, there is no part of your body that has existed for more than 10 years.
lines.translation: 事实上，你身体里没有哪个部分存在的时间超过了10年。

lines.text: “Since our bodies are essentially made anew every few weeks, the material in them alone is clearly insufficient to explain the persistent thread of an identity.
lines.translation: “由于我们的身体基本上每隔几周就会焕然一新，单靠其中的物质显然不足以解释身份的持续性。

lines.text: ” Professor Oliver claims that individualism is actually bad for society, and only by realising we are a part of a bigger entity can we solve pressing environmental and societal problems.
lines.translation: ”奥利弗教授声称，个人主义实际上对社会有害，只有通过意识到我们是一个更大实体的一部分，我们才能解决紧迫的环境和社会问题。

lines.text: Through selfish over-consumption we are destroying the natural world and using non-renewable resources at an accelerating rate.
lines.translation: 通过自私的过度消费，我们正在破坏自然界，并以越来越快的速度消耗不可再生资源。

lines.text: “We are at a critical crossroads as a species where we must rapidly reform our mindsets and behavior to act in less selfish ways,” he said.
lines.translation: 他说：“作为一种物种，我们正处于一个关键的十字路口，我们必须迅速改革我们的心态和行为，以不那么自私的方式行事。”

lines.text: So let’s open our eyes to the hidden connections all around us.
lines.translation: 因此，让我们擦亮眼睛，去发现身边隐藏的联系。

lines.speaker: Q22.
lines.text: What is indeed just an illusion according to Professor Tom Oliver?
lines.translation: 根据汤姆·奥利弗教授的说法，什么确实只是一种幻觉？

lines.speaker: Q23.
lines.text: What does Professor Tom Oliver think of the idea that we exist as independent selves at the center of a subjective universe?
lines.translation: 汤姆·奥利弗教授如何看待我们作为独立的自我存在于主观宇宙中心的观点？

lines.speaker: Q24.
lines.text: Why does Professor Tom Oliver claim that the material “us” is constantly changing?
lines.translation: 为什么汤姆·奥利弗教授声称物质层面的“我们”是在不断变化的？

lines.speaker: Q25.
lines.text: How can we solve pressing environmental and societal problems according to Professor Tom Oliver?
lines.translation: 根据汤姆·奥利弗教授的说法，我们该如何解决紧迫的环境和社会问题？
